company_name: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

company_name: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n

company_name: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic

company_name: Medusa ransomware gang claims attacks on prominent Mississippi hospital, New Jersey county
description: The Medusa ransomware operation has claimed a devastating cyberattack that knocked out systems at the biggest hospital in Mississippi for nine days.

company_name: Georgia man charged for robbing NBA, NFL players through stolen Apple account details
description: A Georgia man who had already served time for conning professional athletes is accused of impersonating a prominent adult film actress in order to break into the Apple accounts of NBA and NFL players.

company_name: Energy Department set to release its first-ever cyber strategy
description: Alex Fitzsimmons, the acting director of the Office of Cybersecurity, Energy Security, and Emergency Response (CESER), said the plan is meant to supplement the recently-published national cyber strategy and will focus on how the agency will strengthen the “security resilience” of the energy sector.

company_name: More Attackers Are Logging In, Not Breaking In
description: Credential theft soared in the second half of 2025, thanks in part to the industrialization of infostealer malware and AI-enabled social engineering.

company_name: Less Lucrative Ransomware Market Makes Attackers Alter Methods
description: Ransomware actors are ditching Cobalt Strike in favor of native Windows tools, as payment rates hit record lows and data theft surges.

company_name: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
description: In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.

company_name: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
description: In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.

company_name: China-Nexus Hackers Skulk in Southeast Asian Military Orgs for Years
description: Researchers uncovered an extensive cyber espionage campaign that used novel backdoors and familiar evasion techniques to maintain persistent access to regional targets.

company_name: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
description: Researchers have identified dozens of malicious GlassWorm extensions that come with new evasion techniques.

company_name: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
description: Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Games from evolving threats.

company_name: Attackers Abuse LiveChat to Phish Credit Card, Personal Data
description: A social engineering campaign impersonating PayPal and Amazon uses customer support interactions to acquire sensitive info.

company_name: The Data Gap: Why Nonprofit Cyber Incidents Go Underreported
description: Threat actors target nonprofits due to security gaps and highly coveted information, but a lack of sufficient data makes it difficult to grasp the entire picture.

company_name: Cyberattackers Don't Care About Good Causes
description: Sightline Security's founder and advisory board discuss how cybersecurity poses significant problems for nonprofits and suggest ways the industry can help.

company_name: Will AI Save Consumers From Smartphone-Based Phishing Attacks?
description: Sophisticated phishing attacks are bypassing on-device protections with troubling frequency, making it more critical than ever for users to protect themselves from potential threats, new research from Omdia shows.

company_name: Most Google Cloud Attacks Start With Bug Exploitation
description: Forget stolen credentials and misconfigurations. Thanks to AI, the new top cause of compromises in the cloud is vulnerability exploits that beat patching cycles.
